paper.title: New Trends in Treating Cushing's Disease

entities.name: New Trends in Treating Cushing's Disease

evidence_entities.text: Rates of recurrence and/or persistence of Cushing's disease after surgical treatment are high.
pasireotide is an approved somatostatin receptor ligand
Cushing's disease (CD) is caused by an adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH)-secreting pituitary adenoma
Levoketoconazole and osilodrostat are novel steroid inhibitors
osilodrostat resulted in a complete response in 66% and a partial response in 9% of the enrolled patients
